question_paper_html:
Annual Exam - 2025

Code: 56/2/3 (set-3)

  1. Please check that this question paper contains 23 printed pages.
  2. Please check that this question paper contains 33 questions.
  3. Q.P. Code given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  4. Please write down the serial number of the question in the answer-book at the given place before attempting it.
  5. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the candidates will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

CHEMISTRY (Theory)

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:70

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions carefully and follow them:

  1. This question paper contains 33 questions. All questions are compulsory.
  2. This question paper is divided into five sections - Section A, B, C, D and E.
  3. Section A - questions number 1 to 16 are multiple choice type questions. Each question carries 1 mark.
  4. Section B - questions number 17 to 21 are very short answer type questions. Each question carries 2 marks.
  5. Section C - questions number 22 to 28 are short answer type questions. Each question carries 3 marks.
  6. Section D - questions number 29 and 30 are case-based questions. Each question carries 4 marks.
  7. Section E - questions number 31 to 33 are long answer type questions. Each question carries 5 marks.
  8. There is no overall choice given in the question paper. However, an internal choice has been provided in few questions in all the sections except Section -A.
  9. Kindly note that there is a separate question paper for Visually Impaired candidates.
  10. Use of calculator is NOT allowed.

Section - A ({tex}16 \times 1=16{/tex})

Question No. 1 to 16 are Multiple Choice type questions carrying 1 mark each.
1.

Which among the following is a false statement?

a. Rate of zero order reaction is independent of initial concentration of reactant.
b. Half-life of a zero order reaction is inversely proportional to the rate constant.
c. Molecularity of a reaction may be zero.
d. For a first order reaction, {tex}{t}_{1 / 2}=0.693 / {k}{/tex}.
1
2.

The charge required for the reduction of 1 mol of {tex}{MnO}_{4}^{-}{/tex} to {tex}{MnO}_{2}{/tex} is

a. 1 F
b. 3 F
c. 5 F
d. 6 F
1
3.

The element having [Ar]{tex}3 {~d}^{10} 4 {~s}^{1}{/tex} electronic configuration is

a. Cu
b. Zn
c. Cr
d. Mn
1
4.

The number of molecules that react with each other in an elementary reaction is a measure of the:

a. activation energy of the reaction
b. order of the reaction
c. stoichiometry of the reaction
d. molecularity of the reaction
1
5.

The diamagnetic species is:
[At. {tex}{No} . {Co}=27, {Fe}=26, {Ni}=28{/tex}]

a. [Ni(CN)4]2-  
b. [NiCl4]2-
c. [Fe(CN)6]3-
d. [CoF6]3-
1
6.

The complex ions {tex}\left[{Co}\left({NH}_{3}\right)_{5}\left({NO}_{2}\right)\right]^{2+}{/tex} and {tex}\left[{Co}\left({NH}_{3}\right)_{5}({ONO})\right]^{2+}{/tex} are called

a. Ionization isomers
b. Linkage isomers
c. Co-ordination isomers
d. Geometrical isomers
1
7.

What will be formed after oxidation reaction of secondary alcohol with chromic anhydride {tex}\left({CrO}_{3}\right){/tex}?

a. Aldehyde
b. Ketone
c. Carboxylic acid
d. Ester
1
8.

Which is the correct IUPAC name for

a. Methylchlorobenzene
b. Toluene
c. 1-Chloro-4-Methylbenzene
d. 1-Methyl-4-Chlorobenzene
1
9.

Which of the following is/are examples of denaturation of protein?

a. Coagulation of egg white
b. Curdling of milk
c. Clotting of blood
d. Both Coagulation of egg white and Curdling of milk
1
10.

The conversion of phenol to salicylic acid can be accomplished by

a. Reimer-Tiemann reaction
b. Friedel-Crafts reaction
c. Kolbe reaction
d. Coupling reaction
1
11.

Scurvy is caused due to deficiency of

a. Vitamin B1
b. Vitamin B2
c. Ascorbic acid
d. Glutamic acid
1
12.

Nucleotides are joined together by

a. phosphodiester linkage
b. peptide linkage
c. glycosidic linkage
d. Hydrogen bonding
1

For questions number 13 to 16, two statements are given - one labelled as Assertion (A) and the other labelled as Reason (R). Select the correct answer to these questions from the codes (A), (B), (C) and (D) as given below:

  1. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true and Reason (R) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  2. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, but Reason (R) is not the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  3. Assertion (A) is true, but Reason (R) is false.
  4. Assertion (A) is false, but Reason (R) is true.
13.

Assertion (A): Cu cannot liberate {tex}{H}_{2}{/tex} on reaction with dilute mineral acids.
Reason (R): Cu has positive electrode potential.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1
14.

Assertion (A): In a first order reaction, if the concentration of the reactant is doubled, its half-life is also doubled.
Reason (R): The half-life of a reaction does not depend upon the initial concentration of the reactant in a first order reaction.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1
15.

Assertion (A): Vitamin D cannot be stored in our body.
Reason (R): Vitamin D is fat soluble vitamin and is not excreted from the body in urine.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1
16.

Assertion (A): Aromatic primary amines cannot be prepared by Gabriel Phthalimide synthesis.
Reason (R): Aryl halides do not undergo nucleophilic substitution reaction with the anion formed by phthalimide.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
1

Name the following coordination compounds according to IUPAC norms:

  1. [Co (NH3)4(H2O)Cl]Cl2
  2. [CrCl2 (en)2]Cl

What is meant by the Rate law and Rate constant of a reaction. Identify the order of a reaction if the units of its Rate constant are:

  2. {tex}{Cr}_{2} {O}_{7}^{2-}+3 {Sn}^{2+}+14 {H}^{+} \longrightarrow{/tex}
 2

The rate constant for a zero order reaction {tex}{A} \rightarrow {P}{/tex} is {tex}0.0030 {~mol} {~L}^{-1} {~s}^{-1}{/tex}. How long will it take for the initial concentration of {tex}A{/tex} to fall from 0.10 M to 0.075 M?

The decomposition of NH3 on platinum surface is zero order reaction. What are the rates of production of N2 and H2 if k = 2.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-4 mol L-1 s-1?

 2

Give reasons for the following observations:

  1. p-Chloronitrobenzene reacts with (aq) NaOH at 443 K to give p-nitrophenol whereas chlorobenzene reacts with the same reagent at 623 K and 300 atm.
  2. Main product obtained when chloroethane reacts with KCN is propane nitrile while with Ag CN it is ethyl isocyanide.

22.

Henry's law constant for {tex}{CO}_2{/tex} in water is {tex}1.67 \times 10^8 {~Pa}{/tex} at 298 K. Calculate the number of moles of {tex}{CO}_2{/tex} in 540 g of soda water when packed under {tex}3.34 \times 10^5 {~Pa}{/tex} at the same temperature.

 3

Give reasons:

  1. Fuel cells are preferred for production of electrical energy than thermal plants.
  2. Iron does not rust even if zinc coating is broken in a galvanized pipe.
  3. In the experimental determination of electrolytic conductance, Direct Current (DC) is not used.

  3. {tex}{Zn}, {Cd}{/tex} and Hg are soft metals. Why?

  1. Using valence bond theory, explain the hybridization and magnetic behaviour of the following:
    1. {tex}\left[{Co}\left({NH}_{3}\right)_{6}\right] {Cl} _3{/tex}

  1. Define the following:

Explain the following reactions and write chemical equation involved:

  1. Wolff-Kishner reduction
  2. Etard reaction
  3. Cannizzaro reaction

Write the structures of A, B and C in the following sequence of reactions:

  1. {tex}{CH}_{3} {COOH} \xrightarrow{{SOCl}_{2}} {~A} \xrightarrow{{H}_{2}, {Pd}-{BaSO}_{4}} {~B} \xrightarrow{{H}_{2} {~N}-{NH}_{2}} {C}{/tex}
  2. {tex}{CH}_{3} {CN} \xrightarrow[2 . {H}_{2} {O}]{1 .({DIBAL}-{H})} {A} \xrightarrow{\text { Dil. } {NaOH}} {B} \xrightarrow{\Delta} {C}{/tex}

Define the following terms:

  1. Native protein
  2. Nucleotide
  3. Essential amino acid

Section - D
29.

The spontaneous flow of the solvent through a semipermeable membrane from a pure solvent to a solution or from a dilute solution to a concentrated solution is called osmosis. The phenomenon of osmosis can be demonstrated by taking two eggs of the same size. In an egg, the membrane below the shell and around the egg material is semipermeable. The outer hard shell can be removed by putting the egg in dilute hydrochloric acid. After removing the hard shell, one egg is placed in distilled water and the other in a saturated salt solution. After some time, the egg placed in distilled water swells-up while the egg placed in salt solution shrinks. The external pressure applied to stop the osmosis is termed as osmotic pressure (a colligative property). Reverse osmosis takes place when the applied external pressure becomes larger than the osmotic pressure.

  1. Define reverse osmosis. Name one SPM which can be used in the process of reverse osmosis. (2)
    1. What do you expect to happen when red blood corpuscles (RBC's) are placed in {tex}0.5 \% {NaCl}{/tex} solution? (1)

      OR

    2. Which one of the following will have higher osmotic pressure in1 M KCl or 1 M urea solution. Justify your answer. (1)
  3. Why osmotic pressure is a colligative property? (1)
 5
30.

Amines have a lone pair of electrons on nitrogen atom due to which they behave as Lewis base. Greater the value of {tex}{K}_{{b}}{/tex} or smaller the value of {tex}{pK}_{{b}}{/tex}, stronger is the base. Amines are more basic than alcohols, ethers, esters, etc. The basic character of aliphatic amines should increase with the increase of alkyl substitution. But it does not occur in a regular manner as a secondary aliphatic amine is unexpectedly more basic than a tertiary amine in aqueous solutions. Aromatic amines are weaker bases than ammonia and aliphatic amines. Electron releasing groups such as {tex}-{CH}_{3}{/tex}, {tex}-{OCH}_{3},-{NH}_{2}{/tex}, etc., increase the basicity while electron-withdrawing substituents such as {tex}-{NO}_{2},-{CN}{/tex}, halogens etc., decrease the basicity of amines. The effect of these substitute is more at {tex}{p}^{-}{/tex}than at {tex}{m}^{-}{/tex}position.

  1. Arrange the following in the increasing order of their basic character.
    Give reason: (2)
  2.  Why {tex}{pK}_{{b}}{/tex} of aniline is more than that of methylamine? (1)
    1. Arrange the following in the increasing order of their basic character in an aqueous solution: (1){tex}\left({CH}_{3}\right)_{3} {~N},\left({CH}_{3}\right)_{2} {NH}, {NH}_{3}, {CH}_{3} {NH}_{2}{/tex}

      OR

    2. Why ammonolysis of alkyl halides is not a good method to prepare pure amines? (1)
 5

Section - E
31.(i)
  1. Calculate the standard Gibbs energy ({tex}\Delta_{{r}} {G}^{\circ}{/tex}) of the following reaction at {tex}25^{\circ} {C}{/tex} :
    {tex}{Au}({s})+{Ca}^{2+}(1 {M}) \rightarrow {Au}^{3+}(1 {M})+{Ca}({s}){/tex}
    {tex}{E}_{{Au}^{3+} / {Au}}^{\circ}=+1.5 {~V}, {E}_{{Ca}^{2+} / {Ca}}^{\circ}=-2.87 {~V}{/tex}
    Predict whether the reaction will be spontaneous or not at 25 {tex}{ }^{\circ} {C}{/tex}.

  2. Tarnished silver contains {tex}{Ag}_{2} {~S}{/tex}. Can this tarnish be removed by placing tarnished silverware in an aluminium pan containing an inert electrolytic solution such as NaCl? The standard electrode potential for half reaction:
    {tex}{Ag}_{2} {~S}({~s})+2 {e}^{-} \longrightarrow 2 {Ag}({s})+{S}^{2-}{/tex} is -0.71 V and for
    {tex}{Al}^{3+}+3 {e}^{-} \longrightarrow 2 {Al}({s}){/tex} is -1.66 V

OR
(ii)
  1. Define the following:
    1. Cell potential
    2. Fuel cell
  2. Calculate emf of the followin; cell at {tex}25^{\circ} {C}{/tex}:
    {tex}\mathrm{Zn}(\mathrm{~s})\left|\mathrm{Zn}_{(0.1 \mathrm{M})}^{2+} \| \mathrm{Cd}_{(0.01 \mathrm{M})}^{2+}\right| \mathrm{Cd}(\mathrm{~s}){/tex}
    Giver : {tex}{E}_{{Cd}^{2+} / {Cd}}^{\circ}=-0.40 {~V}{/tex}
    {tex}{E}_{{Zn}^{2+} / {Zn}}^{\circ}=-0.76 {~V}{/tex}
    [{tex}\log 10=1{/tex}]
 5
32.(i)

An organic compound 'A', molecular formula {tex}{C}_{2} {H}_{6} {O}{/tex} oxidises with {tex}{CrO}_{3}{/tex} to form a compound 'B. Compound 'B' on warming with iodine and aqueous solution of NaOH gives a yellow precipitate of compound 'C'. When compound 'A' is heated with conc. {tex}{H}_{2} {SO}_{4}{/tex} at 413 K gives a compound 'D', which on reaction with excess HI gives compound 'E'. Identify compounds 'A', 'B', 'C', 'D' and 'E' and write chemical equations involved.

 5

OR
(ii)
  1. Write chemical equation of the following reactions:
    1. Phenol is treated with conc. {tex}{HNO}_{3}{/tex}
    2. Propene is treated with {tex}{B}_{2} {H}_{6}{/tex} followed by oxidation by {tex}{H}_{2} {O}_{2} / {OH}^-{/tex}.
    3. Sodium t-butoxide is treated with {tex}{CH}_{3} {Cl}{/tex}.
  2. Give a simple chemical test to distinguish between butan-{tex}1-{ol}{/tex} and butan-2-ol.
  3. Arrange the following in increasing order of acid strength: phenol, ethanol, water.

33.(i)
  1. Give IUPAC name of {tex}{CH}_{3}-{CH}={CH}-{CHO}{/tex}.
  2. Give a simple chemical test to distinguish between propanal and propanone.
  3. How will you convert the following:
    1. Toluene to benzoic acid
    2. Ethanol to propan-2-ol
    3. Propanal to 2-hydroxy propanoic acid
 5

OR
(ii)

Complete each synthesis by giving missing starting material, reagent or products :

Solution:

For zero order reaction,
rate constant, {tex}k=\frac{[R]_{0}-[R]}{t}{/tex}
Given, [R]0 = 0.10 M,
[R] = 0.075 M and
k = 0.0030 mol L-1 s-1
{tex}\therefore \quad t=\frac{[R]_{0}-[R]}{k}{/tex}
{tex}t=\frac{010-0.075}{0.0030}=833 \mathrm{s}{/tex}

The decomposition of NH3 on platinum surface is zero order reaction. What are the rates of production of N2 and H2 if k = 2.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10-4 mol L-1 s-1?

Solution:

{tex}2N{H_3} \to {N_2} + 3{H_2}{/tex}
Rate {tex} = -\frac{1}{2}\frac{{d\left[ {N{H_3}} \right]}}{{dt}} = +\frac{{ d\left[ {{N_2}} \right]}}{{dt}}{/tex}
{tex} = + \frac{1}{3}\frac{{d\left[ {{H_2}} \right]}}{{dt}}{/tex}
For zero order reaction rate = k
{tex}\therefore - \frac{1}{2}\frac{{d\left[ {N{H_3}} \right]}}{{dt}} = \frac{{d\left[ {{N_2}} \right]}}{{dt}} = \frac{1}{3}\frac{{d\left[ {{H_2}} \right]}}{{dt}}{/tex}
{tex} = 2.5 \times {10^{ - 4}}mol\,\,{L^{ - 1}}{s^{ - 1}}{/tex}
Rate of production of {tex}{N_2} = \frac{{d\left[ {{N_2}} \right]}}{{dt}}{/tex}
{tex} = 2.5 \times {10^{ - 4}}mol\,\,{L^{ - 1}}{s^{ - 1}}{/tex}
Rate of production of {tex}{H_2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{3}\frac{{d\left[ {{H_2}} \right]}}{{dt}} = k{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{d\left[ {{H_2}} \right]}}{{dt}} = 3k{/tex}
{tex} = 3 \times 2.5 \times {10^{ - 4}}mol\,{L^{ - 1}}{s^{ - 1}}{/tex}
{tex} = 7.5 \times {10^{ - 4}}mol\,{L^{ - 1}}{s^{ - 1}}{/tex}

The spontaneous flow of the solvent through a semipermeable membrane from a pure solvent to a solution or from a dilute solution to a concentrated solution is called osmosis. The phenomenon of osmosis can be demonstrated by taking two eggs of the same size. In an egg, the membrane below the shell and around the egg material is semipermeable. The outer hard shell can be removed by putting the egg in dilute hydrochloric acid. After removing the hard shell, one egg is placed in distilled water and the other in a saturated salt solution. After some time, the egg placed in distilled water swells-up while the egg placed in salt solution shrinks. The external pressure applied to stop the osmosis is termed as osmotic pressure (a colligative property).
